headline: Fuel Subsidy Rationalisation Saves RM8B But Hurts Millions
context: The government implemented targeted diesel subsidy removal in June 2025 affecting commercial and T20 users, saving RM8 billion annually while retail prices rose from RM2.15 to RM3.35 per litre.

cards.big: Diesel jumped 56% overnight — RM2.15 to RM3.35 — hitting 240,000 truckers first
cards.sub: Transport costs cascade into food prices within 2 weeks of every fuel hike

cards.big: Malaysia spent RM81 billion on fuel subsidies from 2021 to 2024 — more than entire health budget
cards.sub: IMF called blanket subsidies the single biggest drag on Malaysian fiscal health

cards.big: Fishermen in Kelantan report 30% income drop as diesel costs outpace catch revenue
cards.sub: Rural communities without public transport alternatives bear disproportionate burden

cards.big: BUDI Madani cash transfer system covers only 68% of eligible B40 recipients so far
cards.sub: Database gaps mean 1.2 million low-income households still missed by targeting mechanism

cards.big: Subsidy reform is correct policy — but executing it before the safety net works is cruelty by sequence
Should RON95 petrol subsidy removal be delayed until BUDI Madani reaches 95% coverage?
